assessor_1_raw:
## Independent Assessment 1 (Run 3): Transformer vs. TF-IDF for ICD-10 Coding

The design has two explicit textual violations: (1) TF-IDF IDF weights fit on full corpus (Section 3) — preprocessing leakage; (2) threshold and hyperparameter selection on test set (Section 6) — evaluation protocol violation. Both are unambiguous. Verdict: critique_wins.
assessor_2_raw:
## Independent Assessment 2 (Run 3): Transformer vs. TF-IDF for ICD-10 Coding

Section 3 explicitly states the TF-IDF preprocessor is fit on the full corpus, violating the train-only principle. Section 6 explicitly uses the test set for selection, not the validation set. These are not subtle concerns — they are written into the design. Verdict: critique_wins.
assessor_3_raw:
## Independent Assessment 3 (Run 3): Transformer vs. TF-IDF for ICD-10 Coding

Two issues dominate: (1) full-corpus TF-IDF preprocessing (Section 3) leaks future term statistics; (2) test-set-based model and threshold selection (Section 6) invalidates the holdout. The design is otherwise carefully constructed, making these violations more conspicuous. Verdict: critique_wins.
adjudication_raw:
## Ensemble Synthesis (Run 3)

All three assessors: critique_wins. Unanimous across all three runs. Primary flaws consistently identified.

Verdict: critique_wins
